definitions.text: Sufficient; all that is required, needed, or appropriate. Used before a noun in the manner of words like some, a bit of, and so on.
examples: I've already had enough coffee today.
There is food enough for us all.

definitions.text: Used before a noun in the manner of words like some, a bit of, and so on.
examples: I've already had enough coffee today.
There is food enough for us all.
Those pears aren't ripe enough for the children to eat (them).
You've worked enough; rest for a bit.

definitions.text: Fully; quite; used after adjectives to express slight augmentation of the positive degree, and sometimes equivalent to very.
examples: He is ready enough to accept the offer.

definitions.text: Used after certain adverbs to emphasise that a quality is notable, unexpected, etc.
examples: Talking of Mr Smith, funnily enough, I saw him just the other day.
I left my camera on the train, but luckily enough someone handed it in to lost property.

definitions.text: A sufficient or adequate number, amount, etc.
examples: I have enough (of it) to keep me going.
Enough of you are here to begin the class.

definitions.text: Stop! Don't do that any more! It is too much!
examples: I'm sick of you complaining! Enough!

definitions.text: An instance of being sufficient, or of doing something sufficiently.
